taskDescription: Arrange the steps of thyroid gland development in the correct embryological sequence (AN43.4).
paragraphs: Thyroid diverticulum appears as a median endodermal thickening at the foramen caecum of the tongue (between anterior 2/3 and posterior 1/3)
The developing thyroid descends in the midline as a bilobed diverticulum connected to the tongue by the thyroglossal duct
The thyroglossal duct passes anterior to (or through) the body of the hyoid bone during descent
The thyroid reaches its final pretracheal position by the 7th week, and the thyroglossal duct normally obliterates
Failure of duct obliteration leads to thyroglossal cyst (midline swelling that moves with tongue protrusion and swallowing)
